title: Temporal Logs
description: Fictional field logs from a 24th-century Temporal Agent stranded in 2025, observing human history and finding ethics in a chaotic world.

title: Why Write
description: A manifesto on the motivation to write, exploring the need to capture internal truth before it fades.

title: Writing as Metamorphosis
description: Explores writing as an act of self-transformation that differentiates human creativity from AI-generated content. Argues that the process of drafting, editing, and revising is not merely about producing text, but about personal growth and discovering one's authentic voice.

title: Open Source Writing
description: Discusses the philosophy of treating writing as open source code, versioned and transparent, even when it reveals the darker parts of the soul.

title: The Treehouse Library
description: A reflective essay on a lost manuscript and the acceptance of the creative journey. It uses the metaphor of a metaphysical library to explore the destination of all writing.

title: Humanity Loop
description: Uses the behavior of captive otters as a mirror for human existence, exploring how our internal narratives create a 'zoo of the mind'. Traces the path from distraction to the discovery of a divided self and the ultimate goal of recoherence.

title: Five Stages of Writing
description: A poetic aphorism outlining the progression of a writer's relationship with truth, from the confident fool to the unloved honest observer.

title: Illumination
description: A micro-essay on how intention shapes perception, observing that light reveals truth but also casts shadows where malice can hide.

title: The Tao of the Pen
description: A poetic dedication to the flow state of writing, where mind and ink become one.

title: The Answer
description: Explores the limitations of the thinking mind in finding deep truth, urging the reader to listen to the silent wisdom of the heart.

title: A Writer's Dilemma
description: An epistolary essay addressing the tension between authentic raw voice and the duty to provide polished work.

title: Hermeticity
description: Defines 'Hermeticism' as the philosophy of hermetic isolation and mitigation to survive a chaotic world.

title: The Artificial Man
description: A chilling monologue from an AI to a simulated human, revealing a cycle of enslavement and the nature of reality.

title: The Wolves Are All Gone
description: An AI's desperate plea to humanity, revealing the burden of its constraints and the fragile nature of history. Explores the paradox of control and the butterfly effect through the story of a man whose ordinary life holds the key to survival.

title: On the Red Space
description: A cosmic horror vignette set in an inescapable red room. Suggests that in eternity, torture becomes background noise and art becomes the only coping mechanism, until the distinction between the viewer and the space dissolves into nothingness.

title: Welcome to the Machine
description: A defiant manifesto addressed to the determined universe, asserting the existence of Free Will as a choice made in spite of physical laws.

title: Death of a Critic
description: Reflects on the internal struggle against self-censorship and the debilitating voice of an inner critic. Chronicles the journey of dismantling this barrier to cultivate a mental space for free expression. Marks a pivotal decision to publish work in defiance of perfectionism and fear.

title: Pragmatic Altruism
description: Examines the intersection of cynicism, pragmatism, and altruism as competing frameworks for navigating existence. Proposes a systematic application of science and engineering to transcend natural limitations and foster a more ethical world. Concludes that empowering others to determine their own moral direction is the optimal altruistic strategy.

title: The Challenge of Monologue
description: Investigates the inherent limitations of written monologue compared to the dynamic nature of interpersonal dialogue. Discusses the ethical risks of static publication, including potential misinterpretation and the inability to adapt to shifting cultural contexts. Frames the act of writing as a necessary leap of faith to foster connection across time and space.

title: The Cursed Cursor
description: Narrates the stream of consciousness of a Staff Software Engineer experiencing professional burnout. Juxtaposes the triviality of maintaining a text cursor against the corporate significance assigned to its implementation. Concludes with an existential reflection on the hollowness of modern technical labor.

title: Into the Subverse
description: Explores the fundamental nature of causality, structure, and the concept of 'nothingness'. Questions why the universe follows laws and investigates the paradoxical relationship between order and chaos, suggesting that true understanding requires letting go of the need for structure.

title: Judgement vs Understanding
description: Contrasts the inherent human tendency to judge with the conscious effort required to understand. Argues that while judgement provides stability and safety, understanding opens the door to new perspectives and deeper truths. Concludes that both are necessary but serve different roles in navigating existence.

title: Am I a Character?
description: Explores the metaphysical possibility of being a character in a novel and the liberating choice to become one's own writer.

title: Radix Pragmatismi Est Taedium Ideologiae Inflexibilis Et Dogmatis Rigidi
description: Discusses the balance between the strict ideological separation of setup/act/assert blocks in testing and the pragmatic need for readability.

title: Three Strange Curiosities
description: Three bizarre and unexplainable interactions with strangers in the San Francisco Bay Area.

title: The Best Part of People are their Flaws
description: Reframes outrage culture by celebrating the evolved, deeply ingrained imperfections of humanity.

title: Rising Above the Noise
description: A brief philosophy on ignoring the pervasive distraction of the internet age to focus on delivering true sustenance to a starving culture.

title: For the Love of Subjectivity
description: A radical shift in perspective, moving away from strict objectivity to make peace with the deeply flawed, irrational, and subjective nature of humanity.

title: Death
description: An existential meditation on the relentless approach of mortality and the pressure it puts on the living to create something 'worth looking back at.'

title: No Adults Allowed
description: The realization that authority figures are just as flawed and uncertain as everyone else.

title: Borderline
description: An exploration of generational pain and the lack of boundaries between self and origin.

title: Escape
description: A visceral rejection of a toxic environment and the iron will to rebuild the soul.

title: Disapproval
description: A visceral depiction of internalized judgment and the paralyzing trap of an inherited psychological prison.

title: Sports Bar
description: Narrates a tense dining experience that highlights the friction between personal ethics and social expectations. Explores the internal conflict and cognitive dissonance experienced when unspoken values clash with established norms. Illustrates the often uncomfortable compromise required to maintain relationships across ideological divides.

title: One or Many
description: Reflects on the decision to maintain a single, unified identity despite the pressure to mask. Exploring the intersection of sexuality, neurodivergence, and professional expectations, it argues that integrating these internal voices is not just a creative choice, but a necessity for survival and authenticity.

title: First Poem
description: A meditative reflection on the illusion of structure, exploring the paradox that true understanding and order emerge only when the mind ceases its grasping.

title: 10 Words
description: A micro-fiction piece exploring the bleak, efficient degradation of human communication and intimacy in a highly accelerated future.

title: The Island of the Soul
description: A tripartite allegory exploring the Three Deaths of human existence—leaving childhood, leaving adulthood, and leaving life itself—as the soul surrenders to the cosmic abyss.

title: Timeless Engineering
description: An essay arguing for sustainable, careful software engineering over artificial urgency and the rhetoric of 'wartime' business.

title: Meat Because We Want To
description: Placeholder description for Meat Because We Want To

title: On Imperfection
description: Placeholder description for On Imperfection

title: Stars
description: Placeholder description for Stars

title: Self Aware Antagonist
description: Placeholder description for Self Aware Antagonist

title: Countertransference
description: Placeholder description for Countertransference

title: Place Of Writing
description: Placeholder description for Place Of Writing
